authoritative_sources.title: 11140. Transactions in Securities "Ex-Dividend," "Ex-Rights" or "Ex-Warrants"
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 11140 is authoritative for U.S. ex-date handling and is the primary public operating rule constraining record-date, payable-date, and late-information logic in corporate-actions workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Event timing, ex-date, and due-bill aware announcement review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to verify event timing fields explicitly and blocks generic handling that treats all corporate-action dates as interchangeable.

authoritative_sources.title: 6490. Processing of Company-Related Actions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 6490 is authoritative for company-related action notice completeness, timeliness, deficiency handling, and settlement-orderliness concerns in OTC corporate actions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Announcement sufficiency, deficiency, and escalation control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines blocked and escalation behavior when event documentation is incomplete, untimely, or creates uncertainty in settlement and clearance.

authoritative_sources.title: Distributions for Securities - Corporate Actions Processing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC operates the DTC distribution service and is authoritative for full-lifecycle processing of distributions, participant entitlements, instructions, allocations, and payment reporting on DTC-eligible securities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Distribution announcement, entitlement, and payment progression
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains mandatory and elective distribution handling to real DTC-style event states and prevents the lane from treating announcement-only data as completed payment evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Distributions Service Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DTC Distributions Service Guide is an official operating procedure source for CA ID, option blocks, instructions, acknowledgements, entitlements, allocations, adjustments, and ISO 20022 or CA Web processing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Instruction handling, acknowledgements, and allocation traceability
used_for: allocation, adjustment, and participant settlement evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic instruction-submission, acknowledgement, and allocation evidence so the lane can distinguish drafted elections from processed ones.

authoritative_sources.title: Corporate Actions Processing Service for Reorganizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC’s reorganization service description is authoritative for the lifecycle of mandatory and voluntary reorganizations, including instruction processing, entitlement collection, and allocation behavior.
used_for: tender, exchange, conversion, rights, and warrant workflows
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to differentiate distributions from reorganizations and to route voluntary-event authority boundaries correctly.

authoritative_sources.title: 4511. General Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FINRA Rule 4511 is a core books-and-records baseline requiring firms to make and preserve the records needed to support corporate-action execution, exception handling, and closure evidence.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains completion outputs so they require preserved event, instruction, and entitlement records rather than narrative claims about what supposedly happened.

authoritative_sources.title: Books and Records Requirements for Brokers and Dealers Under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This SEC source explains the Rules 17a-3 and 17a-4 books-and-records baseline for preserving the customer, securities, order, and communications records that corporate-actions teams rely on as evidence.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines the named records the lane must request before asserting record truth, exception closure, or completed event handling.

workflow_stages.name: Event intake and authoritative announcement verification
workflow_stages.description: Validate the event identifier, security, event type, record date, ex date, payable or expiration date, option structure, and notice completeness before the event can progress.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Entitlement and eligibility review
workflow_stages.description: Check record-date holdings, due-bill or interim-accounting implications, beneficial-owner eligibility, and source precedence for the event before instructions or allocations are assumed valid.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Election capture, instruction submission, and acknowledgement monitoring
workflow_stages.description: Package client or standing instructions, submit elections through CA Web, ISO 20022, or custodian workflows, and confirm positive acknowledgement before treating the event as instructed.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Allocation, payment, and stock-credit progression
workflow_stages.description: Track entitlements, allocations, adjustments, and settlement-statement effects without treating announcement or instruction states as completed payment or stock-credit outcomes.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling and blocked-state routing
workflow_stages.description: Summarize missing notices, conflicting sources, expired elections, funding failures, withholding issues, and missing allocations with explicit blocker ownership and no unsupported clearance.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence capture and audit-ready closure
workflow_stages.description: Produce completion outputs only when authoritative announcement, instruction, acknowledgement, allocation, payment, stock-credit, and preserved record evidence support closure for the event type.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Issuer, exchange, paying-agent, transfer-agent, custodian, or depository event notices with CA ID or equivalent event identifiers
Record-date position snapshots, due-bill or interim-accounting records, and beneficial-owner eligibility evidence
Option blocks, default and choice options, standing instructions, client or portfolio approvals, and election packages
Instruction messages, positive acknowledgements, instruction-status updates, and cutoff or expiration timestamps
Allocation reports, payment confirmations, stock-credit evidence, participant settlement statements, and adjustment records
Case-management notes, approval history, client or counterparty communications, and retained books-and-records evidence
systems_of_record: DTCC CA Web, CA 20022, or equivalent depository and custodian corporate-actions platforms
Issuer, transfer-agent, paying-agent, exchange, or custodian notice repositories
Position, custody, and portfolio-accounting systems used to establish record-date or eligibility truth
Cash, stock, settlement, tax, and general-ledger systems used to confirm entitlements and downstream postings
CRM, workflow, case-management, and document-management systems
Compliance, legal-escalation, and approval workflow systems

allowed: Determine whether available records support progression, hold, rework, or completion within corporate-actions execution workflows.
Produce execution summaries, completion evidence, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to named event, option, instruction, allocation, and payment records.
Identify when corporate-action blockers require the next operational owner, approver, or adjacent specialist.
escalate: A legal, tax, issuer, client-authorization, or cross-border market factor changes the risk posture.
A source-precedence or event-timing interpretation materially changes whether the request may proceed.
Announcement, instruction, position, allocation, or payment records conflict and need human review or adjacent-owner resolution.
refuse: The request asks for investment advice, client election choice, legal interpretation, tax advice, issuer approval, or final compliance signoff outside the lane.
The request asks to waive a cutoff, override a control, or mark work complete without authoritative evidence.
Required tenant facts, market context, or system-of-record evidence are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Treating record date, ex date, payable date, expiration date, and effective date as interchangeable and progressing the wrong event step.
Submitting or narrating an election using issuer text rather than the authoritative CA ID, option number, and workflow record used by the servicing platform.
Marking a voluntary event complete without positive acknowledgement, valid funding, or final allocation evidence.
Treating announcement or instruction presence as proof of payment, stock credit, or participant settlement completion.
Collapsing issuer, custodian, depository, and internal position or tax records into one narrative state when they materially conflict.
Assuming all corporate actions are simple cash dividends and ignoring reorganizations, due bills, rights-subscription funding, ADR handling, or foreign-market differences.
Drifting into portfolio-decision authority, proxy voting, legal or tax interpretation, issuer services, or settlement-fail ownership.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction and market-infrastructure regime outside the default U.S. DTC-oriented baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Non-DTC, non-U.S., ADR, or global-custody flows can change event timing, election handling, tax treatment, and evidence expectations materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant-provided jurisdiction, market, and custodian-chain context before progression; if absent, return blocked or route to a more specialized lane.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Beneficial-owner election authority and approval posture
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Tenants differ on when operations may release an election, when portfolio-manager or client approval is needed, and how standing instructions may be used.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant authorization policy before acting on voluntary events and refuse to infer election authority when approval context is missing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source precedence between issuer notices, exchange notices, DTCC or custodian notices, and tenant event stores
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Different environments give priority to different event sources when deadlines, option text, or eligibility details disagree.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant source-precedence policy before clearing conflicts or claiming a final event state.

notes: The source set combines FINRA corporate-action timing and notice rules, DTCC lifecycle workflow references for distributions and reorganizations, and FINRA or SEC books-and-records control standards.

The pack is intentionally corporate-actions-specific: it centers event notices, option structures, elections, acknowledgements, entitlements, allocations, and completion evidence instead of generic finance operations wording.
No blocking ambiguity remains because open points are translated into tenant-required facts, explicit refusal conditions, or adjacent-lane routing rules.
